other_observers: I was the only person there at that time.
description:
To find
the bird I hiked almost all the way around the pond to where I could hear and
see American Robins. It was still quite foggy, and the birds were not up and
about yet. In the northeast corner of the riparian area, I spotted the Rufous-backed
Robin sitting near the center of a Russian Olive Tree that still had quite a
few berries. The bird was all puffed up. Originally found by Coen Dexter and
Brenda White, many birders flocked to Cortez to see this bird.Bird description
– the same size and shape as an American Robin, this bird was much more rufous.
The back, coverts, and scapulars were orangish red as well as the belly. The
tail, primaries, secondaries, and head were cold gray. The throat was a dirty
white with fine vertical black streaks that faded into the rufous. This robin
had a bicolored yellow and gray bill with black in front of the eye. The
auricular patch was slightly darker than the crown and lacked any white around
a dark eye. Before
the bird took flight with the rest of the American Robins it was associating
with, it did look like it had a ‘hair ball’ that it might be trying to dislodge.
Nothing came out. I didn’t hear any vocalizations unfortunately. The bird
rustled its feathers and took flight to the NW side of the pond.
photographer: Photographer: Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe
